title: They Said Buy Shopify at $140. The AI Ran the Numbers.
description: A popular financial publication recommended Shopify as a top Canadian tech stock. We audited the recommendation with data. Here's what they missed.
verdict: Grade: C+. Decent company, wrong entry. They ignored the 85x forward P/E and the slowing merchant growth rate. Three better Canadian tech plays existed at the time.

content.headline: They Said Buy Shopify at $140. The AI Ran the Numbers.
content.summary: A major financial publication told readers to buy Shopify (TSX:SHOP) at around $140 CAD, calling it a 'must-own Canadian tech stock.' Our AI audited the recommendation and found three critical blind spots in their analysis — including ignoring a forward P/E of 85x while better-valued alternatives existed.
content.foolClaim: Shopify is a must-own Canadian tech stock. The e-commerce giant continues to innovate with AI-powered tools and its merchant base is growing. Buy and hold for the long term.

candidates.reasonConsidered: The stock under review. Strong brand, dominant in SMB e-commerce, AI features launching. But at $140 CAD, trading at ~85x forward earnings with decelerating merchant growth (29% YoY vs 33% prior quarter).

Canada's largest software company. Trading at 12x forward earnings, 6% dividend yield, and just completed the Micro Focus integration ahead of schedule. Enterprise AI features driving upsell. Way better value than SHOP at this entry.
Supply chain AI pure-play. Growing 20%+ with improving margins. At 45x forward P/E — expensive but half of Shopify's multiple with more defensible enterprise contracts.
The GOAT of Canadian software. Serial acquirer with a flawless track record. Trading at 30x earnings which is its historical average — fair value for a compounder.
AI-powered LMS platform. Interesting AI angle but small-cap with thin trading volume.
candidates.reasonEliminated: Average daily volume under 100K shares on TSX. Liquidity risk for retail investors. Good company, bad trade.

content.analysis:
**The core problem with this recommendation isn't Shopify itself — it's the entry price.**

Shopify is a great company. Nobody disputes that. It dominates SMB e-commerce, it's building genuinely useful AI tools (Shopify Magic, Sidekick), and its merchant ecosystem is a moat. The publication got all of that right.

**But here's what they didn't tell you:**

At $140 CAD, Shopify trades at approximately 85x forward earnings. That means you're paying for nearly a decade of perfect execution. One earnings miss, one guidance cut, and you're looking at a 15-25% drawdown. That's not a 'buy and hold' setup — it's a 'pray nothing goes wrong' setup.

**The merchant growth deceleration is a red flag they ignored entirely.** Q4 2025 showed 29% YoY merchant growth, down from 33% the prior quarter and 38% a year ago. The law of large numbers is kicking in, and the publication's thesis of 'growing merchant base' is technically true but directionally misleading.

**What they should have recommended instead:**

OpenText (OTEX) is arguably the better Canadian tech play right now. At 12x forward earnings with a 6% dividend yield, you're getting enterprise software exposure at a fraction of Shopify's valuation. The Micro Focus integration is complete, margins are expanding, and the AI upsell story is real — but priced like it might not work, not like it definitely will.

Kinaxis (KXS) is another option: supply chain AI with 20%+ growth and enterprise contracts that don't churn. At 45x forward P/E it's not cheap, but it's half of Shopify's multiple with arguably more defensible revenue.

**The bottom line:** Recommending Shopify without discussing the 85x multiple, the merchant growth deceleration, or any alternative isn't analysis — it's brand recognition dressed up as due diligence.
risks: They ignored the 85x forward P/E — any earnings miss means a 15-25% drawdown
Merchant growth deceleration (29% vs 33% prior quarter) was not mentioned
No discussion of alternatives at better valuations
No entry price discipline — 'buy and hold' without a price target is lazy
Currency risk for Canadian investors buying USD-denominated revenue exposure
catalysts: AI tool rollout (Shopify Magic, Sidekick) — real but already priced in at 85x
Merchant base growth — true but decelerating, not accelerating
E-commerce secular trend — valid long-term, but not unique to Shopify
content.finalVerdict: Grade: C+. Shopify is a great company at a mediocre entry price. The publication's analysis was surface-level — strong on narrative, weak on valuation. They should have flagged the 85x multiple, acknowledged the growth deceleration, and at minimum compared SHOP to OTEX and KXS as alternatives. Recommending a stock without discussing price is like recommending a house without mentioning it costs $2M. Not financial advice — but if you're going to follow someone's stock picks, demand they show their work.
